data.epi_content:
Not long after, Vivian really did come to my room.

"Um, Erica! It's me, Vivian.

I was worried about you, so I came to check on you. Could you please open the door?"

I felt a sense of déjà vu.

Well, it had already happened before, but I was so tired from everything I'd been through that my brain might have tucked it away in some corner, not wanting to think about it anymore.

I approached the entrance and silently opened the door.

There stood a familiar pretty girl with blonde hair, greeting me with a flustered expression.

"Uh, um, h-hello?"

Right, she was the one I'd slapped and parted ways with a few days ago.

Saying we "parted ways" makes it sound like we were lovers, but we were just friends, or something like that.

I don't think I ever really had friends anyway.

If she was going to be this surprised that I opened the door, she probably shouldn't have knocked in the first place.

"Uh, um..."

While I was thinking this, I must have been staring at her without responding to her greeting, because she made an uncomfortable sound.

"Come in. I don't know why you're here, though."

I offered her a chair and brought out some chocolate and tea that Evan had brought me—or more accurately, that I had asked him to bring. Since I had long since consumed all the tea and coffee I had in my room.

Offering refreshments to guests is basic etiquette no matter where you are.

"Sorry, I don't know what you like, so I just brought out what I enjoy."

"N-no! It's fine!"

If she says so.

"I see."

Then silence fell between us.

I didn't want to become close with her, didn't want to share common interests, and I certainly wasn't in the mood to chat and laugh together.

I had never been happy being involved with this girl.

I hadn't been particularly happy being involved with Evan either, but the fact that there had been at least a few moments was what mattered.

Specifically, when I had become a cripple with missing limbs and eyes, crawling on the floor like a worm, he had helped me successfully meet my death.

Perhaps the girl in front of me couldn't bear the silence.

She sat demurely and handed me a small paper bag she was holding in her left hand.

I didn't take it.

She cleared her throat and placed it on the table.

"Well, I thought I shouldn't come empty-handed, so I baked some cookies."

"You could have come empty-handed for all I care.

I'm not some nasty pig at the Imperial Palace who needs to be buttered up with gifts."

Vivian looked slightly surprised at my words.

"So why did you come?"

I didn't order her to leave like last time.

Considering that "last time" was almost a few years ago, I must have grown quite a bit over the past few years.

I think I can be proud of that.

"I heard someone tried to harm you, and I overheard some classmates gossiping.

Since you haven't been coming to class, I was worried something might have happened to you..."

That "someone" has been reduced to dust and washed down the drain.

Why was I so genuinely troubled back then?

No, I had reason to be troubled.

It hurt.

I should have run away.
data.epi_content2:
Why did I just helplessly endure it?

Let's just say I was stupid.

Laughing now doesn't really fit the situation.

It's time to return to being the stubborn, obstinate, foolish young lady Erica.

"Heheh. Ha, sorry. It's just funny."

My laughter made Vivian flustered.

Thinking I might change the mood, I took out a long black cigar from my chest, put it in my mouth, and fidgeted with it.

Good quality cigars taste better when lit with a strong flame.

But since there's a magician more skilled than Evan right in front of me, I might as well take advantage.

"Would you mind lighting this for me?"

Vivian looked slightly perplexed, but she dutifully lit it for me.

"Thank you. Would you like to smoke one?"

"Ah, no! I'm fine!"

"So you came because you were worried?"

At that, Vivian nodded and stretched out her words as she answered, "Yes."

"There's nothing to worry about.

I can stay in the dormitory at the Academy as much as I want for now, and when they tell me to leave, I'll leave! That's all.

And then I'll go to Evan... oh."

Maybe I shouldn't bring up Evan.

As I was thinking this, my eyes met Vivian's.

I hated that look in her eyes—pitying me yet showing a hint of complexity.

When Lydia looked down on me, crushing me and trying to erase me, I could simply brush it off. But looking at those eyes filled me with a mix of love and hate.

Thoughts like: Why did she do such terrible things while liking me? Why didn't she hate me? Why did she confine me?

In that moment, I threw the glass I was holding against the wall.

I've become so experienced at throwing glasses that I could be called a professional, successfully creating a crisp shattering sound.

Some fragments flew and embedded themselves in my skin, causing a little bleeding, but that was a side effect I could handle.

"If you came because you were worried, why don't you leave now?

For the sake of our amicable relationship."

"I did come because I was worried, but I'm not leaving. I came to make up and become close friends again like before."

After saying that, Vivian silently began picking up the glass fragments scattered on the floor.

Another familiar scene.

After finishing cleaning the floor with a spell, she walked to the balcony.

Then she pulled back the closed curtains.

Dazzling light began to fill the room.

And the sight of the girl smiling in that light looked so natural.

As if she should naturally receive such light, naturally be so beautiful, and naturally be so charming. The light surrounding her illuminated her face, making it impossible to feel confident about one's own appearance—but even this thought was something I had already had before.

It's not déjà vu; it's simply the same events happening again.

I think at this point, I couldn't say anything to Vivian as she cleaned up the glass fragments, and I just trembled and asked her to leave.

Instead, I was just taking in the sight of this beautiful woman crawling on the floor, cleaning my room.

"Um, should I call you 'young lady'?"

What did I answer back then?

"There's no need to call me 'young lady' anymore."

Why didn't I need to be called "young lady"?

Ah, I had completely fallen apart!

"Haven't you heard? About what happened to my family."
data.epi_content3:
"But you're still a young lady to me.

From the first time we met, that's exactly what I thought.

Do you remember what I said when we first met?"

I don't remember.

"With all the difficult things I've been through lately, I can hardly be expected to remember such details."

At those words, Vivian hesitated slightly before continuing the conversation.

"I asked you to be my friend, and you nodded."

If you say so, then I guess that's what happened.

"When I first entered the Academy, you showed me around.

When there were parts of the lessons I didn't understand, you and Evan would explain them to me, or show me how to solve problems, things like that."

Even if she recounts all these good stories and memories, she should know there's no possibility of going back to those times.

But perhaps I can pretend, just for a very brief moment.

After all, Evan said that while Vivian might be defeated with words, he seemed quite afraid of her.

"And then we drifted apart a bit..."

Yes, we drifted apart.

Vivian stopped mid-sentence and closed her mouth.

I quite enjoy awkward silences.

I used to find them uncomfortable too, but now I don't feel discomfort anymore.

Experience is truly helpful.

Since I've lived a few more years than Vivian, I decided to speak first.

Awkward silence aside, having someone in my room, especially someone who brings back unpleasant memories, doesn't exactly make me happy.

"So, do you want to be friends with me?

Like the other students at this school who chat together, eat delicious food together, sometimes sleep in the same room, things like that?

Burying everything that happened between us and acting as if nothing ever happened?"

Pretending I never tried to kill myself.

Forgetting the memories of being locked in a room, trying to escape because I felt like I would die from the suffocation, only to be caught and tied up.

Being taken to the dining hall and given delicious food, gratefully crying while devouring the feed like an animal.

Like a doll being hugged in bed, I should be moved by the grace even if it's suffocating.

Pushing all of that aside.

I stood up.

Vivian's face showed slight disappointment as she listened to me.

"Well, why not?

What good would it do to stand on pride at this point?"

Right.

I didn't need to consider Vivian's feelings.

It's all up to her.

If she would just not pay attention to me, not torment me, not stop me, the problem would be solved.

As always, pretending not to see.

Even if someone dies.

"Fine, let's reconcile.

Since I'm much lower in status, should I call it an apology?

I'm sorry for slapping you before, Vivian."

"No, suddenly, that's... that's not what I..."

As I said this, I approached Vivian and gave her a slight hug.

The flustered Vivian stopped speaking and hiccupped slightly.

I whispered in the ear of this beautiful woman.

"I'd like to be alone now, so please leave, Vivian."

Come to think of it, she was in no position to worry about anyone.
